title: I started a Quant Fund
author: eggrally
selftext:
I have recent started a quant fund, it's handsoff , ai makes the trade,  it's all daytrade,  nothing held overnight, all stocks no futures or crypto. It's a strategy  I'v been manually trading for years.  But ai does it better.  The new fund is trading real money.  The ai is slowly scaling up. It's a new beginning for 

"Ai Alpha Quant Fund"

https://preview.redd.it/rcfjvxt5kc2h1.jpg?width=1568&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=e4acf0285d132a07087c5e6ca32f209af3ad35b7

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: This is my own fund,  so no link

comments.author: Slight_Ad_6375
comments.body: Awesome, congrats! What’s the alpha and sharpe?

comments.author: Affectionate_Fill189
comments.body: What broker do you execute through?

comments.author: No_Side2315
comments.body: What makes it a "quant" fund? Is the AI analyzing the market data directly, making position size/trade decisions and placing buy/sell orders itself or is it writing its own code and managing research?

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: computer algorithms to drive its investment decisions, rather than human judgment

comments.author: EfficientJeweler1430
comments.body:
Interesting since its real money and fully hands off, the part I’d be most curious about is the validation layer.

Just few qst pls do u track every trade from signal to execution? Like AI decision, entry reason, expected price, actual fill, slippage, broker response, exit reason, and whether the trade matched what the model expected?

The p&l curve looks good, but for an ai driven fund the real question is whether the live execution can be audited trade by trade!

replies.author: eggrally
replies.body: Ai follows the plan that I gave it,  ai watches the market and executes when everything lines up.
